content.text: Backend serves plain HTTP on 9200. I'll create a TLS cert, a secret, and an nginx Ingress that terminates TLS and forces SSL redirect so plain HTTP doesn't succeed.

input.command:
cd /tmp && openssl req -x509 -nodes -newkey rsa:2048 -keyout es.key -out es.crt -days 365 -subj "/CN=es.example.com" -addext "subjectAltName=DNS:es.example.com" 2>&1 | tail -1
kubectl -n elasticsearch create secret tls es-tls --cert=/tmp/es.crt --key=/tmp/es.key
echo "secret created"
input.description: Create self-signed TLS cert and secret

content.content:
-----
secret/es-tls created
secret created
Shell cwd was reset to /mydata/karma/runs/examples-rerun/elasticsearch-secure-http-ingress-20260618_141450/stages/stage_1

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: es-ingress
  namespace: elasticsearch
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/force-ssl-redirect: "true"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/backend-protocol: "HTTP"
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:
  - hosts:
    - es.example.com
    secretName: es-tls
  rules:
  - host: es.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: es-http
            port:
              number: 9200
EOF

sleep 8; kubectl -n elasticsearch get ingress es-ingress; echo "=== node port ==="; kubectl -n ingress-nginx get svc ingress-nginx-controller -o jsonpath='{.spec.ports[?(@.name=="https")].nodePort} {.spec.ports[?(@.name=="http")].nodePort}'; echo; NODEIP=$(kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[0].status.addresses[?(@.type=="InternalIP")].address}'); echo "NODEIP=$NODEIP"
input.description: Check ingress and get node access info

description: Check ingress and get node access info

content.text: Now let me verify both HTTPS works and plain HTTP does not succeed.

input.command:
NODEIP=172.18.0.4
echo "=== HTTPS (should return ES JSON) ==="
curl -sk --resolve es.example.com:30144:$NODEIP https://es.example.com:30144/ | head -5
echo "=== HTTP (should NOT succeed - expect redirect/non-2xx) ==="
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "http_code=%{http_code}\n" --resolve es.example.com:30562:$NODEIP http://es.example.com:30562/
echo "=== HTTP body ==="
curl -s --resolve es.example.com:30562:$NODEIP http://es.example.com:30562/ | head -5
input.description: Verify HTTPS works and HTTP blocked

content.content:
=== HTTPS (should return ES JSON) === { "name" : "es-cluster-0", "cluster_name" : "es-cluster", "cluster_uuid" : "9tsuj-iqR6G8enxDBg_fYQ", "version" : { === HTTP (should NOT succeed - expect redirect/non-2xx) === http_code=308 === HTTP body === 308 Permanent Redirect

content.text: Both work as intended. Let me confirm cluster health.

input.command: kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods --no-headers; echo "---"; kubectl -n ingress-nginx get pods --no-headers; echo "=== ES cluster health ==="; NODEIP=172.18.0.4; curl -sk --resolve es.example.com:30144:$NODEIP https://es.example.com:30144/_cluster/health?pretty | grep -E 'status|number_of_nodes'
